epic.description: Tasks extracted from [Sprint-Planning][20260205] Login Page Improvement.

MAT-3569.description:
_SP:_ 4

2. Context: need to evaluate available WhatsApp Business API providers (e.g. Twilio and alternatives) to determine the most suitable option for our system  
   3. Todo:  
      1. research available WAB providers (personal accounts may be used for evaluation)  
      2. compare based on:  
         1. SLA and uptime guarantees  
         2. reliability and message delivery  
         3. pricing and cost model  
         4. API documentation quality (must be accessible via HTTPS)  
         5. ease of integration with our existing system  
      3. investigate fallback mechanisms (if a primary provider becomes unavailable, whether multiple providers can be used)  
      4. list env needed for next tasks (e.g. auth token, SID for account identifier, etc)  
      5. Can check [PRD \[US-9\] WhatsApp OTP](https://docs.google.com/document/d/11fFbFR2WBKDAV1jecko91HeUJThTYNxjdEcQuFU0tAQ/edit?disco=AAABzUbzLDo) for further requirements  
   4. AC:  
      1. SPIKE docs  
   5. Deps:  
      1. \-

MAT-3571.description:
_SP:_ 0

2. Context: with the things we store in this sprint, redis might end up consuming all memory. hence we need to adjust redis config they have maxmemory config and maxmemory-policy config  
   3. Todo:  
      1. Implement volatile-ttl (evict keys with an associated expiration, TTL, that have the shortest remaining TTL value)  
      2. Refer to [docs](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/reference/eviction/)  
   4. AC:  
      1. Able to implement eviction of keys based on shortest TTL  
   5. Deps:  
      1. \-

MAT-3572.description:
_SP:_ 1

2. Context: need to create tables and package  
   3. Todo:  
      1. Create migration based on [technical breakdown](?tab=t.ih3v4ftggs44)  
         1. Create blacklisted\_login\_logs table  
         2. Adjust user table

         4. Populate last\_password\_reminder\_at with updated\_at

         1. Separate all layers into different packages (authhandler, authmodel, etc)  
         2. Create the constructors for each layers

      2. Package created accordingly  
   5. Deps

MAT-3573.description:
_SP:_ 1

![][image2]

   2. Context: need to create new email template  
   3. Todo:  
      1. Create dynamic template in sendgrid  
         1. Refer to this [design](https://www.figma.com/design/CPoA9hPdIIricEshBgDktH/Login-Wall?node-id=1182-5363&t=AJ6YMYSWARGjpgZ9-4)  
         2. Params  
            1. {{user\_name}}  
            2. {{whatsapp\_number}} ΓåÆ construct [wa.me](http://wa.me) link from here  
            3. {{otp\_code}}  
      2. Add template to our [email template repo](https://github.com/Match-Made/email-template-generator)   
      3. Add env var to fusion and populate the value in github actions  w/ sendgrid Template ID (APP\_EMAIL\_LOGIN\_OTP\_TEMPLATE\_ID)  
   4. AC:  
      1. Login OTP email template created  
      2. Template ID is ready to use in fusion  
   5. Deps  
      1. \-

MAT-3577.description:
_SP:_ 1

2. Context: we need to enable passwordless sign in  
   3. Todo  
      1. Endpoint: POST /login  
      2. Request:

         {

           "email": "user@example.com",

           "password": "string"

         }

      3. Response: { firebase user obj }  
      4. Hit firebase for sign in with password ([docs](https://docs.cloud.google.com/identity-platform/docs/reference/rest/v1/accounts/signInWithPassword))  
      5. Adjust hola\_client package as well  
   4. AC:  
      1. Able to login using email and password  
   5. Deps:

MAT-3578.description:
_SP:_ 2

2. Context: we currently hit firebase endpoint directly from FE. now, we need to have an endpoint to handle login to replace that flow  
    3. Todo  
       1. Endpoint: POST /auth/v1/login  
       2. Request: 

          {

            "email": "user@example.com",

            "password": "string"

          }

       3. Success response:: 

          {

            "mfa\_required": \<APP\_FT\_MFA\_REQUIRED\> and user.mfa\_required,

            "token\_id": "", ΓåÆ only if mfaRequired \= true

             ΓÇ£firebase\_userΓÇ¥: {user obj} ΓåÆ only if mfaRequired \= false

          }

       4. Failed response:

          {

            "error": "",

            "failed\_count": 3

          }

       5. Details:  
          1. Hit hola POST /login  
             1. If APP\_FT\_MFA\_REQUIRED \= false OR user.mfa\_required \= false, return resp immediately (skip steps below)  
          2. On success login  
             1. Generate random token\_id  
             2. Store firebase resp object in redis  
                1. Key: **auth:login:token:{token\_id}**  
                2. TTL: APP\_FIREBASE\_USER\_OBJECT\_TTL (10 minutes)  
             3. Reset failed login counter **auth:login:fail:{email}**  
          3. On failed login  
             1. Get failed counter  
                1. Key: **auth:login:fail:{email}**  
             2. If counter \= 10  
                1. Return 403 id blocked  
             3. If counter not exists OR counter \< 10  
                1. Increment failed counter in redis  
                2. Reset TTL using APP\_FAILED\_LOGIN\_COUNTER\_TTL (30 minutes)  
             4. Return current failed count to FE  
             5. If failed count after incremented \== 10:  
                1. Call RevokeRefreshTokens function to force log out  
                2. Send email (use APP\_EMAIL\_ACCOUNT\_LOCKED\_TEMPLATE\_ID)  
                   1. Params:  
                      1. {{user\_name}}  
                      2. {{whatsapp\_number}} ΓåÆ construct [wa.me](http://wa.me) link from here  
                      3. {{date\_time}}  
                3. Log to blacklisted\_login\_logs (ip addr, email)  
          4. Error codes & messages:  
             1. Counter \>= 10  
                1. 403 \- ID Blocked  
             2. Wrong email pass combo  
                1. 400 \- Invalid Creds  
       6. Refer to [flow diagram](?tab=t.ih3v4ftggs44) for overall flow and handling both success & error cases  
    4. AC:  
       1. Able to successfully login all types of user using password and email  
       2. Able to increment failed counter on error  
       3. Able to lock user and send email on 10th err  
       4. When FT is false returns firebase object for direct login  
    5. Deps:

MAT-3582.description:
_SP:_ 2

2. Context: we need to generate OTP using email and be able to send them to user  
    3. Todo:  
       1. Endpoint: POST /auth/v1/otp  
       2. Request: 

          {

             ΓÇ£emailΓÇ¥: ΓÇ£ΓÇ¥,

             ΓÇ£phone\_numberΓÇ¥: ΓÇ£ΓÇ¥,

             ΓÇ£mfa\_channelΓÇ¥: ΓÇ£emailΓÇ¥

             ΓÇ£token\_idΓÇ¥: ΓÇ£ΓÇ¥, ΓåÆ optional

          }

       3. Response:

          {

            "token\_id": "", 

          }

       4. For email  
          1. Mfa\_channel \= ΓÇ£emailΓÇ¥  
          2. Email is required  
          3. Phone\_number is not needed  
       5. Details:  
          1. Enforce daily OTP limit counter  
             1. Key: **auth:otp:daily:{email}**  
             2. TTL: end of day  
             3. If \>20 return 429  
             4. If not, increment and continue steps below  
          2. Generate OTP for email  
             1. 6-digit numeric code  
          3. If token\_id is not in request, generate random token\_id  
          4. Store OTP in redis using token\_id  
             1. Key: **auth:otp:{token\_id}**  
             2. TTL: 2 minutes  
             3. Overwrite existing OTP if any to invalidate old ones  
          5. Send OTP to email (use APP\_EMAIL\_LOGIN\_OTP\_TEMPLATE\_ID)  
             1. Params:  
                1. {{user\_name}}  
                2. {{otp\_code}}  
                3. {{whatsapp\_number}} ΓåÆ construct [wa.me](http://wa.me) link from here  
          6. Return token\_id  
       6. This task should only handle email  
       7. Make sure that the c.iii functions are reusable so that we can use them in login with email pass endpoint  
    4. AC:  
       1. Able to generate OTP and send them to user  
       2. Able to enforce 20 OTP per day per account limit  
    5. Deps

_SP:_ 1

2. Context: we need to adjust login with email password endpoint to also generate and send OTP on success  
    3. Todo:  
       1. On success login  
          1. Use previously created functions to generate and send OTP before returning  
          2. Always use email first after login

       1. Able to generate OTP and send them to user when login using valid email/pass

MAT-3591.description:
_SP:_ 2

2. Context: we need to be able to verify OTPs based on previously generated ones  
    3. Todo:  
       1. Endpoint: POST /auth/v1/login/verify-otp  
       2. Request: 

          {

            "token\_id": "",

            "otp": "123456",

            ΓÇ£mfa\_channelΓÇ¥: ΓÇ£emailΓÇ¥/ΓÇ¥whatsappΓÇ¥,

          }

       3. Response: 

          {

              "kind": ΓÇ£"

              "localId": ΓÇ£ΓÇ¥

              "email": ΓÇ£ΓÇ¥

              "displayName": ΓÇ£ΓÇ¥

              "idToken": ΓÇ£ΓÇ¥

              "registered": ΓÇ£ΓÇ¥

              "refreshToken": "",

              "expiresIn": ""

          }

       4. Details:  
          1. Retrieve OTP  
             1. Key: **auth:otp:{token\_id}**  
          2. Verify  
             1. Check OTP matches stored OTP  
          3. Get stored user obj  
             1. Key: **auth:login:token:{token\_id}**  
          4. On success  
             1. Delete:  
                1. Previously fetched **auth:otp:{token\_id}**  
                2. All auth:login:\*  
                   1. **auth:login:token:{token\_id}**  
                   2. **auth:login:fail:{email}**  
             2. If mfa\_channel \= ΓÇ£whatsappΓÇ¥, send email (use APP\_EMAIL\_SUCCESSFUL\_LOGIN\_TEMPLATE\_ID)  
                1. Params:  
                   1. {{user\_name}}  
                   2. {{whatsapp\_number}} ΓåÆ construct [wa.me](http://wa.me) link from here  
                   3. {{date\_time}}  
                   4. {{location}}  
                   5. {{device\_type}}  
                   6. {{ip\_address}}  
                   7. {{reset\_password\_link}} ΓåÆ use holaclient  
             3. Return obj from (iv.3)  
          5. Make sure that the c.iv.1 and c.iv.2 functions are reusable so that we can use them in verify otp for update phone number  
    4. AC:  
       1. Able to verify OTP  
    5. Deps

MAT-3594.description:
_SP:_ 2

![][image6]

    2. Context: need to add new submit OTP page on our reactor  
    3. Todo:  
       1. Refer to [design](https://www.figma.com/design/CPoA9hPdIIricEshBgDktH/Login-Wall?node-id=1182-1580&t=AJ6YMYSWARGjpgZ9-4)  
       2. Should consist:  
          1. Error states  
          2. Microinteractions (hover, disable, etc)  
          3. Request new OTP timer (2 minutes) after clicking  
       3. Does not consist:  
          1. Endpoint integration  
          2. Flow integration (due to this, please create a separate temporary button in login modal as entry point for QA testing)  
       4. Use [shadcn input OTP](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/radix/input-otp) (user should be able to copy paste OTP directly)  
       5. For contact support use VITE\_WHATSAPP\_NUMBER (copy from ForgotPasswordForm)  
    4. AC:  
       1. Page styling and microinteractions are according to Figma  
    5. Deps  
       1. \-

MAT-3595.description:
_SP:_ 1

2. Context: need to adjust the behavior of our login page to hit BE instead of directly hitting firebase  
    3. Todo  
       1. Implement FT to protect route VITE\_FT\_LOGIN\_IMPROVEMENT  
          1. If false, use the current flow (reactor hit firebase directly)  
          2. If true, proceed with flow below  
       2. Hit POST /auth/v1/login  
          1. On error, show labels/toast accordingly  
          2. On success  
             1. If mfaRequired \= true redirect to OTP page  
             2. Else redirect to dashboard and set user based on firebaseUser  
       3. Hit POST /auth/v1/otp  
          1. When click request new OTP  
          2. Use token\_id from (c.ii)  
       4. Hit POST /auth/v1/login/verify-otp  
          1. Req: submitted otp, mfa\_channel, and token\_id from (c.ii)  
          2. Set user based on response  
       5. Need to adjust the way we store our user object  
          1. Make sure post-login analytics is not removed  
    4. AC:  
       1. Able to login with new flow using email password  
    5. Deps:

MAT-3600.description:
_SP:_ 1

2. Context: some prod users have unverified emails in firebase, seen [here](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1YdcxSDDgp5kM3pGCaUo3G3OIeR9SgPPnMcUxj-qo5qk/edit?usp=sharing). We need to set emailVerified \= true for these existing users so they can use both magic link and email pass login  
    3. Todo:  
       1. For each user in our firebase project  
          1. Check the emailVerified value  
          2. If false, then update the user so that emailVerified \= true  
    4. AC:  
       1. Able to set emailVerified \= true for all users in a firebase project   
    5. Deps  
       1. \-

MAT-3623.description:
_SP:_ 2

2. Context: need to be able to update user details  
    3. Todo:  
       1. Endpoint: PATCH /api/v1/users/:id  
       2. Request: 

          {

              verification: {

                  current\_password: ΓÇ£ΓÇ¥,

                  otp: ΓÇ£ΓÇ¥,

                  token\_id: ΓÇ£ΓÇ¥,

              },

              updated\_data: {

                  phone\_number: ΓÇ£ΓÇ¥,    

                  password: ΓÇ£ΓÇ¥,    

                  last\_password\_reminder\_at: ΓÇ£ΓÇ¥,    

              }

          }

       3. Response: { user object }  
       4. Verification object  
          1. current\_password: used to try signing in to check legitimacy  
          2. token\_id: used to get stored otp  
          3. otp: used to compare with the stored otp  
       5. On change phone number  
          1. All verification fields are required  
             1. Try signing in using current\_password  
             2. Try comparing the submitted otp with the stored otp (get auth:otp:{token\_id})  
          2. Update phone\_number\_changed\_at  
          3. Will use 62 (or \+62) ΓåÆ based off of SPIKE  
       6. On change password  
          1. Only current\_password field is required in verification  
             1. Try signing in using current\_password  
          2. Hit holaclient UpdateUser  
          3. Update password\_changed\_at  
          4. If successfully updated, call RevokeRefreshTokens function to force log out  
       7. Updates can be partial  
    4. AC:  
       1. Able to update user details accordingly  
    5. Deps

MAT-3627.description:
_SP:_ 2

2. Context: we need to be able to update user account from web  
    3. Todo:  
       1. Show user info in the account page  
          1. Hit GET /users/:id  
       2. Upon requesting OTP  
          1. Hit POST /auth/v1/otp  
             1. Use phone\_number  
             2. If user re-request OTP, make sure to use the firstly received token\_id from this endpoint in request body  
       3. Upon changing phone number  
          1. Hit PATCH /users/:id  
          2. Fill the verification object  
             1. current\_password  
             2. OTP  
             3. token\_id (from [c.ii](http://c.ii))  
       4. Upon changing password  
          1. Hit PATCH /users/:id  
          2. Fill the verification object  
             1. current\_password  
       5. Make sure this is only shown to user-typed user  
    4. AC:  
       1. Able to change password  
       2. Able to change phone number and verify it  
    5. Deps

          1. Hit PATCH /users/:id  
          2. Fill the verification object  
             1. current\_password  
             2. OTP  
             3. token\_id (from [c.ii](http://c.ii))  
       4. Upon changing password  
          1. Hit PATCH /users/:id  
          2. Fill the verification object  
             1. current\_password  
       5. Make sure this is only shown to user-typed user  
    4. AC:  
       1. Able to change password  
       2. Able to change phone number and verify it  
    5. Deps

2. Context: need to know when the last time a user has been updated  
    3. Todo:  
       1. Upon opening web/after logging in, hit GET /users/:id  
       2. If last\_password\_reminder\_at is \>= 90 days ago, show modal  
       3. This modal will persist like TnC until user clicks ΓÇ£Remind me laterΓÇ¥  
       4. Upon clicking ΓÇ£Update passwordΓÇ¥  
          1. Hit PATCH /users/:id  
             1. last\_password\_reminder\_at \= today  
          2. Redirect to account detail page with password change pane opened  
       5. On click ΓÇ£remind me laterΓÇ¥
